claim: Tenerife October daily highs decrease from 27°C (81°F) to 26°C (78°F) across the month with cloud cover rising from 28% to 38%.
quote: In Tenerife during October average daily high temperatures decrease from 81°F to 78°F and the fraction of time spent overcast or mostly cloudy increases from 28% to 38%.

claim: Tenerife averages 25°C daytime highs in October with about 10 mm of rain over ~4 days and 7-8 sunshine hours.
quote: During October the average high temperature in Tenerife is 25ºC... Expect minimal rainfall in Tenerife during October, with a total of 10 mm over approximately 4 days.

claim: Tenerife sea temperature peaks at 23-24°C from August to October, warm enough for swimming.
quote: The sea temperature ranges from 19 °C (66 °F) in February and March to 23/24 °C (73/75 °F) between August and October, when it can be considered warm enough for swimming.

claim: Gran Canaria October averages 24°C high, 20°C low with stable temperatures and ~11 hours daily sunlight.
quote: The temperature is extremely stable in October with average highs of 24°C and lows just 4°C lower at 20°C... an average of 11 hours of daily sunlight.

claim: Gran Canaria October sea temperature averages 22°C, the year's warmest, with only ~13 mm rain over ~9 days.
quote: The average sea temperature is 22°C, and it's the warmest the sea will be all year... There's only 13 mm of precipitation, that falls over an average of nine days in October.

claim: Lanzarote October highs drop from 27°C to 25°C, with ~11 mm rain over 4 days, 9 sunshine hours and 22°C sea.
quote: In October, the average temperature in Lanzarote drops from 24°C at the beginning of the month down to 22°C in the final few days, with daily highs going from 27°C down to 25°C... Lanzarote receives an average of 11mm of rainfall spread across four days in October... average sea temperature for Lanzarote is around 22°C.

claim: Fuerteventura October averages 25°C daytime with sea around 22.5°C; regional highs 24-26°C.
quote: In October, Fuerteventura enjoys an average daytime temperature of 25°C, with the sea temperature around 22.5°C in September and October.

claim: Funchal Madeira October averages 24.5°C highs early month falling to 22.9°C late, with sea cooling 23°C to 22.2°C.
quote: early in the month, highs average 24.5°C (76.1°F), and lows are 21°C (69.8°F), while by month's end, average highs are 22.9°C (73.2°F)... Funchal experiences gradual warming of the water in October at 23°C (73.4°F) in the first third, 22.8°C (73°F) mid-month, and 22.2°C (72°F) by month's end.

claim: Malta October sees high rainfall with 85+ mm/month and the wet season starts; daytime highs around 24°C.
quote: October marks the beginning of the wetter season for Malta, as from October to January rainfall is equal to or higher than 85 mm (3.3 in) per month.

claim: Malta October daily highs decline from 26°C (79°F) early month to 22°C (72°F) by month-end - borderline for ≥20°C late October.
quote: During October average daily high temperatures decrease from 79°F to 72°F as the month progresses.

claim: Paphos Cyprus October averages 27°C daytime high, 16°C night, 18 mm rain over 5 days, sea 24.5°C.
quote: Average high temperature: 27°C, Average nighttime temperature: 16°C, Rainfall: 18mm spread over five wet days, Average sea temperature: 24.5°C (76.1°F)

claim: Cyprus in October has ~10 hours daily sunshine and sea temps still above 20°C, suitable for swimming.
quote: In October, the average sea temperature exceeds 20°C, making it pleasant for swimming... there are about 10 hours of continuous sunshine per day.

claim: Heraklion Crete October averages 24.3°C high, 17.8°C low, 95 mm rain, sea 23°C, 6 sunshine hours.
quote: The average temperature is 21°C/69.8°F (maximum: 24.3°C/75.8°F | minimum: 17.8°C/64°F). There are 6 hours of sunshine per day on average with typically 95 mm of rainfall in the month. The mean sea temperature is 23°C/73.4°F.

claim: Chania Crete October averages 23.9°C high, 16.8°C low - borderline late October as cooler weather sets in.
quote: The average temperature is 20.1°C/68.1°F (maximum 23.9°C/75.1°F | minimum 16.8°C/62.2°F). The month of October is a transitional period in Chania since warm weather is slowly replaced by cold breezes and some rainfalls.

claim: Rhodes October starts warm at 25-27°C early then milder; autumn becomes rainier as month progresses.
quote: At the beginning of the month, daytime temperatures often reach around 25° to 27°C, while evenings are pleasantly mild at 18° to 20°C... autumn, from mid-September to November, is pleasantly warm at the beginning, then gradually becomes milder and rainier.

claim: Rhodes October averages 24°C max, 17°C min, 68 mm rain over 5 days, sea 23°C, 8 sunshine hours.
quote: Average daytime maximum temperatures reach 24°C in Rhodes in October, with minimum temperatures around 17°C... Average rainfall amounts to 68mm over five rainy days... average sea temperature is 23°C... There are 8 hours of sunshine per day on average.

claim: Kos October averages 26°C high, 18°C low, ~59 mm rain over 6 days, sea 23°C - autumn becomes milder/rainier through month.
quote: The average temperature is 22°C/71.6°F with maximum 26°C/78.8°F and minimum 18°C/64.4°F... approximately 59mm of rain spread over six days... average temperature of 23°C... autumn... gradually becomes milder, while the rains become more frequent.

claim: Catania Sicily October is the wettest month with 105-113 mm rain, average highs 23°C, 7 sunshine hours.
quote: October is the wettest month in Catania. Precipitation amounts to 105 mm, distributed over 7 days... average high temperature is 23.2°C (73.8°F)... There are on average 7 hours of sunshine per day in October.

claim: Palermo October daily high 24.3°C, low 17.5°C, 80 mm rain over 8 days, sea 22.4°C, 7 sunshine hours.
quote: Daily high: 24.3°C; Daily low: 17.5°C; Total monthly rainfall: 80 mm; Rainy days: 8; Sea Temperature: 22.4°C... 7 hours per day

claim: Faro Algarve October highs drop from 25°C to 22°C across the month, ~70 mm rain over ~8 days, sea ~19°C.
quote: Daytime temperatures generally settle around 22°C, while nighttime can see a dip to about 17°C. Temperatures in Faro typically drop from around 25°C at the start of October to approximately 22°C by the end... averaging around 70 mm for the month... 8 days with some rain.

claim: Málaga October highs 24-25°C with first half particularly warm/sunny; ~45-57 mm rain over ~5 days.
quote: October stands out as a month of moderate rainfall with 45 mm (1.8 in) of rainfall spread over just 5 days... In October, there are still many warm and sunny days, especially in the first half.

claim: Seville October highs drop from 29°C to 24°C across the month, with ~72 mm rain over 10 days; first rains begin.
quote: In October, Seville has daytime highs of 27°C (81°F), with temperatures typically dropping from around 29°C (84°F) at the start of October to approximately 24°C (75°F) by the end... approximately 10 days in October are expected to have rain, averaging 72 mm.

claim: Athens October averages 23.8°C high, 15.9°C low, 45 mm rain over 5 days, sea 22°C, 6.5 sunshine hours - borderline late October.
quote: average temperature of 19.9°C, with a minimum of 15.9°C and a maximum of 23.8°C. Precipitation amounts to 45 mm, distributed over 5 days... average sea temperature is 22°C... approximately 6.5 hours of sunshine per day. By mid-October, the likelihood of rainfall increases.

claim: Peloponnese October temperatures range 20-25°C, comfortable conditions across the region.
quote: During October, you can expect comfortable weather, with average temperatures ranging from 20°C to 25°C.

claim: Faro October averages 22°C daytime, 17°C night, with sea temperature around 19°C - borderline for ≥20°C late October.
quote: Daytime temperatures generally settle around 22°C, while nighttime can see a dip to about 17°C... The water temperature sits at about 19°C.

claim: Brussels Airport (BRU) will offer direct flights to 180 destinations in 2026 with 70 airlines, season starting March 29.
quote: Brussels Airport will offer direct flights to 180 destinations this summer, with 70 airlines operating during the 2026 season starting March 29.

claim: Ryanair operates 10 weekly flights from Charleroi (CRL) to Tenerife South (TFS) with average flight time 4h30, sole operator on the route.
quote: There are 10 flights per week flying from Brussels Charleroi to Tenerife (Reina Sofía) (as of January 2026). The average flight time is 4 hours and 30 minutes.

claim: Charleroi (CRL) has Ryanair direct service to Tenerife, Gran Canaria, Lanzarote, Fuerteventura, Madeira, Faro, Paphos, Heraklion, Chania, Rhodes, Athens, Catania, Palermo, Málaga and Sevilla; Athens also served by Pegasus.
quote: non-stop passenger flights scheduled to 129 destinations in 30 countries

claim: Aegean is the only carrier flying nonstop from Brussels (BRU) to Larnaca (LCA), with 5 weekly flights as of January 2026 and a flight time of around 4 hours.
quote: there are 5 flights per week flying from Brussels to Larnaca (as of January 2026)... only one airline operating non-stop flights from Brussels BRU to Larnaca LCA, which is Aegean.

claim: Brussels Airlines operates direct flights from BRU to Heraklion (HER), Crete.
quote: Flight from Brussels (BRU) to Heraklion - Crete (HER) | Brussels Airlines

claim: Brussels Airlines operates 8 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Heraklion with average flight time 3h20 (April 2026).
quote: Brussels Airlines operates nonstop flights from Brussels to Heraklion (Crete), with 8 flights per week as of April 2026. The average flight time is 3 hours and 20 minutes.

claim: BRU to Funchal (Madeira) has 3 weekly nonstop flights with average duration 4h30; Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly are the operators.
quote: There are 3 flights per week flying from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira) as of April 2026. Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly are flying nonstop from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira). The average flight time from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira) is 4 hours and 30 minutes.

claim: Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly fly direct from BRU to Catania (CTA), seasonal April–November.
quote: You can fly non-stop to Catania with Brussels Airlines (Star Alliance) or TUI Fly, as a seasonal route that starts in April and ends in November.

claim: TUI fly Belgium operates 2 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Paphos (PFO) with flight time 4h25; Ryanair also serves the route.
quote: There are 2 direct flights between Brussels and Paphos every week, operated by TUI fly Belgium with a flight time of 4 hours and 25 minutes. Additionally, Ryanair connects Brussels and Paphos with direct flights to Paphos airport.

claim: Ryanair is the sole nonstop carrier from Charleroi (CRL) to Malta (MLA), operating 9 weekly flights with departures spread 07:05–21:50.
quote: Ryanair is the only airline flying nonstop from Brussels Charleroi to Malta. Flights from CRL to Malta are operated 9 times a week, with an average of 1 flight per day, and departure times vary between 07:05 - 21:50.

claim: Ryanair flies CRL to Málaga (AGP) 11 times weekly (about 2 daily), sole operator on the route.
quote: Flights from CRL to Málaga are operated 11 times a week, with an average of 2 flights per day, and departure times vary between 06:40 - 22:00. Ryanair is the only airline flying nonstop from Brussels Charleroi to Málaga.

claim: CRL to Faro (FAO) drops to 1 weekly Ryanair flight by March 2026 (off-season), sole operator.
quote: There is 1 flight per week flying from Brussels Charleroi to Faro (as of March 2026). Ryanair is the only airline operating non-stop flights from Brussels Charleroi CRL to Faro FAO.

claim: BRU to Tenerife South (TFS) has 11 weekly nonstop flights with average flight time 5 hours; Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TUI Fly all operate the route.
quote: There are 11 flights per week flying from Brussels to Tenerife (Reina Sofía) (as of April 2026), with an average flight time of 5 hours. Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TUI Fly are flying nonstop from Brussels to Tenerife.

claim: BRU–Athens has ~25–26 weekly direct flights operated by Brussels Airlines, Aegean and SAS, with flight time approximately 3h20.
quote: There are 25 flights per week flying from Athens to Brussels (as of April 2026)... Brussels Airlines, Aegean Airlines and Scandinavian Airlines fly direct from Athens International to Brussels.

claim: Brussels Airlines runs 9 weekly nonstop flights BRU–Málaga (AGP) with flight duration 2h55; Vueling also serves the route.
quote: Brussels Airlines is the most popular for nonstop flights on this route, offering 9 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP)... flight duration of 02:55 hours.

claim: Brussels Airlines flies BRU–Faro (FAO) with 6 weekly nonstop services.
quote: Brussels Airlines is the most popular for nonstop flights, offering 6 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Faro International Airport (FAO).

claim: Brussels Airlines operates ~2 weekly direct flights from BRU to Chania (CHQ) with flight time 3h25.
quote: Brussels Airlines operates approximately 2 weekly flights from Brussels to Chania with a flight duration of 03:25 hours.

claim: Brussels Airlines flies from BRU direct to Athens, Heraklion, Chania, Kos and Rhodes.
quote: Flights to Greece | Brussels Airlines

claim: Ryanair operates the CRL–Malta route with flight time 2h55.
quote: The route between Malta and Brussels South Charleroi Airport is operated by Ryanair with a flight time of 2 hours and 55 minutes.

claim: Brussels Airlines confirms a direct service from BRU to Málaga.
quote: Flight from Brussels (BRU) to Málaga (AGP) | Brussels Airlines

claim: Vueling operates direct flights from Brussels to Málaga.
quote: Cheap flights Brussels to Malaga | Vueling

claim: Transavia operates ~4 weekly nonstop flights BRU to Seville (SVQ); Brussels Airlines does not serve this route directly.
quote: Transavia is the most popular airline for nonstop flights to Seville, offering 4 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Seville Airport (SVQ).

claim: Tenerife's signature attraction is Mount Teide, Spain's highest peak at 3,718 m, accessible by cable car with day, sunset, or stargazing tours; Teide National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the most visited national park in Europe.
quote: You can enjoy a guided trip to Teide by day, at sunset or under a blanket of stars, and you can even ascend to the peak of the highest mountain in Spain by hiking to an altitude of 3,718 m.

claim: A typical week in Tenerife splits between north (La Laguna, Anaga laurel forests, Puerto de la Cruz) and south (resorts, Siam Park, Los Gigantes cliffs), with central Teide as a day trip — combining volcano, forests, towns, and beaches.
quote: Anaga Rural Park... misty forests, twisting laurel trees, and a magical, almost prehistoric atmosphere... often called the 'Enchanted Forest'.

claim: Tenerife is the most varied and lively Canary, known for all-inclusive resorts, beaches and clubbing, drawing 10+ million annual visitors; Gran Canaria feels like a 'little continent' with dramatic terrain variation appealing to adventure seekers.
quote: Tenerife is renowned for its all-inclusive resorts, beaches and lively clubbing scene... Gran Canaria is the island that feels most like its very own little continent, thanks to its wide and dramatic variation of terrain.

claim: Gran Canaria's signature sight is the 404-hectare Maspalomas Dunes Nature Reserve, a Sahara-like dune system with beach, palm grove and brackish La Charca lagoon hosting up to 24 migratory bird species; camel rides and the Costa Canaria promenade are key activities.
quote: a 400-hectare protected Nature Reserve includes a fantastic beach, a dynamic sand dune system, palm grove and a brackish lagoon.

claim: Gran Canaria pairs beach/nightlife in Maspalomas-Playa del Inglés with mountain villages like Teror and lush hill attractions like Palmitos Park, plus the Las Palmas capital — fast contrasts in one week.
quote: Teror is one of the most beautiful villages in Gran Canaria, famous for its centuries-old architecture and the Basilica of Nuestra Señora del Pino.

claim: Lanzarote's headline attraction is Timanfaya National Park — a Mars-like landscape of 100+ volcanoes featuring a coach-only Lunar Route, geyser/fire demonstrations, and the El Diablo restaurant where food is grilled by geothermal heat.
quote: As soon as you enter the park, you feel like you've landed on another planet: Mars, with breathtaking landscapes of volcanic craters, lava fields, fumaroles, all in shades of red, purple, and black.

claim: Lanzarote is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve where César Manrique's art-architecture (Jameos del Agua, Mirador del Río, Cactus Garden) is fused with the volcanic landscape; a classic day pairs Timanfaya morning with La Geria volcanic-vineyard lunch.
quote: The classic Lanzarote day involves going early to Timanfaya National Park for the best flow, then driving through La Geria (volcanic vineyards) and stopping for a slow lunch.

claim: Fuerteventura's defining feature is empty beaches and world-class windsports: Sotavento has 9 km of sand with flat waist-deep water and consistent side-shore wind, drawing half of Europe's kitesurfers; Cofete is a remote wild Atlantic beach.
quote: Sotavento is why half the kitesurfers in Europe know Fuerteventura exists, with a massive stretch of sand running for about nine kilometres along the southeast coast, creating flat, waist-deep water with consistent side-shore wind.

claim: Fuerteventura highlights include Corralejo Natural Park dunes, Isla de Lobos day trip (entry pass required, keeps crowds down), El Cotillo surf, and Oasis Park zoo/botanical garden — quieter and less developed than Tenerife or Gran Canaria.
quote: Isla de Lobos is a beautiful island close enough to Fuerteventura for a day trip that can only be reached by ferry, and you need an entry pass to get on the island, which helps keep crowds down.

claim: Madeira's signature activity is the levada walks — irrigation-channel trails through Laurisilva forest; from January 2026 mandatory pre-booking is required for popular PR trails via the SIMplifica platform.
quote: From January 1, 2026, mandatory hiking reservations are needed for all popular PR trails, and all hikers are required to pre-book a time slot via the government's SIMplifica platform.

claim: Top Madeira hikes include Levada do Caldeirão Verde (PR9) ending at a waterfall, 25 Fontes & Risco at Rabaçal, Vereda dos Balcões (PR11), and the Ponta de São Lourenço peninsula (PR8); September–November is recommended as ideal hiking season.
quote: The best time for Levada walks in Madeira is spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November), when the weather is mild, the trails are less crowded, and the landscapes are especially vibrant.

claim: A week in Madeira typically combines Funchal city base (markets, cable car to Monte, toboggan ride) with day trips to Pico Ruivo summit, Caldeirão Verde, Câmara de Lobos and the western Rabaçal/Porto Moniz natural pools; pace is slow and nature-focused.
quote: Madeira has 1,400 kilometres of levadas — irrigation channels carved into the mountains over 500 years, now turned into some of Europe's finest walking trails that run through UNESCO laurel forests, past waterfalls, through hand-carved tunnels.

claim: Malta's capital Valletta packs St John's Co-Cathedral (Caravaggio), Upper Barrakka Gardens above Grand Harbour, Fort St Elmo, and Casa Rocca Piccola into a tiny, walkable UNESCO peninsula; cruise crowds arrive mid-morning so go early.
quote: Start sightseeing early in Valletta as cruise ship crowds arrive mid-morning.

claim: Mdina, the 'Silent City', is a fortified medieval hilltop former capital of narrow alleys and Baroque palaces, best visited at sunset when light hits the limestone and day-tour crowds leave; pairs naturally with Rabat catacombs.
quote: Mdina is a maze of narrow alleys and Baroque noble palaces with an almost mystical silence as soon as you enter.

claim: A typical Malta week covers Valletta, Mdina/Rabat, Marsaxlokk Sunday fish market, the Three Cities, Blue Grotto, prehistoric Ħaġar Qim/Mnajdra temples (older than Stonehenge) and a Gozo day trip via 25-min ferry from Cirkewwa.
quote: The Neolithic Temples of Ħaġar Qim and Mnajdra on the southern coast were built around 3600–3200 BC, making them older than Stonehenge and the Egyptian pyramids.

claim: Paphos signature sights are the UNESCO Kato Paphos Archaeological Park (Roman mosaics) and Tombs of the Kings, plus Avakas Gorge hike, Aphrodite nature trail, and Blue Lagoon day trips; harbour promenade is the evening focal point.
quote: Ancient Roman mosaics at the UNESCO-listed Kato Paphos Archaeological Park and Tombs of the Kings are among the town's top historical finds.

claim: Larnaca is more compact, beach-and-bar focused with palm-lined Finikoudes promenade and easy reach of Ayia Napa nightlife (30 min); Paphos has more historic sites and outdoor activities but weaker beaches — the two are 1.5 hours apart by car.
quote: Paphos is the city for you if you're an active traveller or if you're looking for a great mix between outdoor activities, beach time, and visiting historical sites... Larnaca is lacking in sites and it is far more suited for days spent lounging on the beach rather than exploring.

claim: Chania (western Crete) is widely considered the most beautiful city in Crete with a Venetian harbour, Ottoman mosque, and old town; it's the base for Samaria Gorge (open May–October), Elafonisi pink-sand beach, Falassarna and Balos.
quote: The Samaria Gorge is the most popular hiking trail in Crete—an 18-km-long gorge open from May to October and located on the southern side of Chania.

claim: Heraklion suits travellers prioritising Knossos Palace and the Archaeological Museum, and is better positioned to reach central/eastern Crete (Lassithi, Phaistos, Matala); Chania is more touristy/picturesque while Heraklion feels more authentic and working-Greek.
quote: Heraklion is in the centre of Crete with public transport connections to much of the island, whereas Chania is in the west so you're only going to be able to see the western half of the island from there.

claim: A week-long Chania-base itinerary covers Old Town, Samaria Gorge full-day hike, Elafonisi/Falassarna beach days, Akrotiri peninsula monasteries, Loutro south-coast village, and Rethymno old town day trip.
quote: Chania features a charming medieval town with some of the most beautiful beaches in Greece, intriguing castles and a unique natural landscape.

claim: Rhodes' signature is the UNESCO Medieval Old Town — the oldest inhabited medieval city in Europe — with the Street of the Knights, Palace of the Grand Master, and pedestrian-only walls; Lindos village + acropolis and St Paul's Bay are the main south-island draw.
quote: When you enter the walls of the Medieval Old Town of Rhodes, you enter into the oldest inhabited medieval city in Europe!

claim: A standard Rhodes week adds a Symi catamaran day trip (1 hr each way, May 16–Oct 31 daily), Tsambika and Anthony Quinn Bay beaches, Valley of the Butterflies, and the Acropolis of Lindos; October offers 20–25°C and lighter crowds.
quote: From May 16 to October 31, ferries depart daily on the King Saron catamaran and arrive in Symi in 65 minutes.

claim: Sicily in October centres on Mount Etna (grape harvest at 700 m, summit fumaroles), Valley of the Temples, Palermo street food, Trapani salt pans with flamingos, and Syracuse/Ortigia; daily highs around 25°C with quieter sites but ferry sailings to Aeolians/Egadi shrink mid-month.
quote: By October, the daily high settles around 25°C (77°F), comfortable enough to spend two hours on the ridge at the Valley of the Temples without clock-watching for shade.

claim: A classic Sicily week hits Palermo, San Vito Lo Capo, Agrigento, Ragusa Ibla/Ortigia, Taormina (cliff-top with ancient theatre views to Etna), Mount Etna, and Cefalù — busy day-tripping pace with significant driving between regions.
quote: A popular 7-day itinerary includes: Day 1 Palermo, Day 2 San Vito Lo Capo, Day 3 Agrigento area, Day 4 Ragusa Ibla/Marzamemi/Ortigia, Day 5 Ortigia & Taormina, Day 6 Mount Etna, and Day 7 Alcantara Gorge & Cefalù.

claim: Algarve highlights are Ponta da Piedade cliffs/sea caves (boat or kayak), Praia da Marinha, Benagil cave, Lagos Old Town, Cape St Vincent, and Ria Formosa lagoon near Faro; pace is slow-beach with golf and hiking add-ons.
quote: Ponta da Piedade features golden cliffs and sea caves best seen by boat tour, though kayaking gets you closer and the clifftop path offers excellent views.

claim: October in the Algarve is shoulder-season with ~23°C highs, sea around 19°C, lush green landscapes, up-to-30% lodging discounts, autumn bird migration, and Faro's Feira de Santa Iria; quieter beaches and ideal for hiking, cycling, and golf.
quote: This shoulder-season month is ideal if you prefer smaller crowds and milder temperatures, with average daily highs of around 23°C (73°F).

claim: Málaga's top sights are the Alcazaba Moorish fortress, Picasso Museum, Gibralfaro castle for sunset, Cathedral, and 40+ museums; signature experiences include espetos (sardine skewers) on the beach, flamenco, and the Caminito del Rey clifftop walk in the province.
quote: Activities include wandering tapas bars, climbing Gibralfaro for sunset, and embarking on adventure along the King's Little Path, a unique pathway against the sheer cliffs of a narrow ravine in the Málaga province.

claim: Málaga/Costa del Sol pairs city culture with Costa del Sol beaches (Marbella, Nerja, Torremolinos) and inland white villages like Mijas and Ronda; October is part of the recommended September–November shoulder window with mild weather and uncrowded beaches.
quote: The best time to visit Málaga is spring (March-May) or fall (September-November), when the weather is mild and the beaches aren't overcrowded.

claim: Forum consensus: Larnaca is preferred by travellers wanting compact walkable beach-bar atmosphere with good food; Paphos is preferred for sightseeing and a more 'tourist-resort' setup with better hotel quality but weaker beaches.
quote: People quite like Larnaca because it is compact with lots of bars and restaurants within easy reach with some sandy beaches. In contrast, Paphos is much more 'tourist' area have much more very nice hotels but unfortunately its beaches are not as good as other towns.

claim: Reviewer consensus: Malta is worth a full week — small enough to see comprehensively, big on history with megalithic temples, walled cities, and a Gozo day trip; rental car strongly recommended for flexibility.
quote: If you only have a week in Malta, it's a good length of time to see the best bits and more, as Malta is a small island and you can see a surprising amount in one week.

claim: Brussels–Tenerife flights start around $147 round trip, with TUI fly, Transavia and Brussels Airlines as primary direct operators.
quote: Cheap flights from Brussels (BRU) to Tenerife (TFS) start at $77 for one-way and $147 for round trip

claim: Tenerife mid-range restaurant meal for two with drinks runs €35–60; menu del día €9–13.
quote: Mid-range restaurant meals for two (including drinks) cost €35-€60. Menu del Día costs €9-13

claim: Average mid-range hotel in Tenerife costs about $84 per night, weekly average ~$580 excluding tax.
quote: for the 808 mid-range hotels in Tenerife, the average price is $84 with high season prices around $157, and the median price is $81

claim: Car rental in Tenerife runs roughly €300–450 per week for a small/mid-sized car.
quote: you should budget €300-450 per week for a small to mid-sized car

claim: Brussels–Madeira (Funchal) round-trip flights start from around £76, with Ryanair from Charleroi the cheapest direct option.
quote: Return flights start from £76

claim: Ryanair Brussels Charleroi to Madeira can be found from around €56 round trip in shoulder season.
quote: Cheap non-stop flights from Brussels Charleroi to Madeira ☀ from €56

claim: Funchal mid-range dining is cheap by Portuguese standards (~26% lower than Lisbon); single-person living cost ~€1,200/mo signals reasonable food prices.
quote: Restaurant prices in Funchal are 25.8% lower than in Lisbon

claim: Madeira's signature levada trails now charge €4.50 per person (€10.50 for Pico do Areeiro–Pico Ruivo) from 2026.
quote: Most levada trails cost €4.50 per person for independent hikers... Pico do Areeiro–Pico Ruivo costs €7 via protocol operators and €10.50 for the general public

claim: Brussels–Malta direct round-trip fares start around £29 with Ryanair, Brussels Airlines and KM Malta operating the route.
quote: round-trip tickets start from £29 and one-way flights from Brussels to Malta start from £15

claim: Malta mid-range restaurant dinner costs €25–40 per person; Valletta is one of the more expensive areas with rents €1,100–1,600/mo.
quote: A dinner at a mid-range restaurant in Valletta costs €25–40 per person, and a bottle of wine at a restaurant adds €18–25

claim: Brussels Airlines lists Brussels–Larnaca starting around €322; typical RT averages near €700 making Cyprus the most expensive flight on the shortlist.
quote: Brussels Airlines offers travel from Brussels to Larnaca starting at just 322 EUR

claim: Cyprus mid-range hotels (2–3 stars) run $90–175 per night double, with Paphos cheaper than Limassol.
quote: mid-range hotels (2–3 stars) with a standard double room cost between $90 and $175 per night

claim: Brussels–Heraklion round-trip from $187, with TUI fly direct service averaging $207; October Ryanair fares from $59 one-way.
quote: Cheap flights from Brussels to Heraklion start at $105 for one-way and $187 for round trip

claim: Rhodes mid-range hotel runs €70–130 per night, week of accommodation €490–770, mid-range food €35–50/day per person.
quote: A good 3–4* hotel or apartment costs around €490–€770 for one week (around €70–€110 per night)

claim: Brussels–Rhodes Ryanair direct from Charleroi runs April–October, fares from ~€122 round trip; October is among cheapest months.
quote: Ryanair offers flights from Brussels to Rhodes starting from $147 (€122)... October is the cheapest month

claim: Sicily three-star hotels run €60–95/night in shoulder season; mid-range dining €60–90/day per person.
quote: Three-star hotels throughout Sicily deliver reliable comfort... at €90-140 nightly for double occupancy during peak summer 2026, dropping to €60-95 in shoulder seasons

claim: Brussels–Sicily (Palermo/Catania) round-trip fares typically $98–200; Lufthansa from €146 to Catania.
quote: Cheap flights from Brussels to Palermo... Lufthansa offers flights from Brussels to Catania starting at 146 EUR

claim: Brussels–Faro round-trip fares start as low as $51, with Ryanair, Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TAP all serving the route.
quote: $51 CHEAP FLIGHTS from Brussels to Faro

claim: Algarve mid-range three-course meal for two ~€40, inexpensive main €12; cost of living moderate among Portuguese regions.
quote: A simple main course at an inexpensive restaurant averages about €12, while a three-course meal for two at a mid-range place is around €40

claim: Brussels–Málaga round-trip from $56 on Ryanair/Brussels Airlines; 4 weekly direct frequencies, ~2h50 flight.
quote: $56 CHEAP FLIGHTS from Brussels to Málaga

claim: Belgian autumn (Toussaint) school break runs 19 Oct–1 Nov 2026 (Flemish/German) and 2–7 Nov 2026 (French) — these dates spike flight prices ex-Belgium.
quote: Autumn Break (All Saints' Day) runs from Monday, October 19 to Sunday, November 1, 2026

claim: Lanzarote/Fuerteventura mid-range accommodation runs €60–100/night; mid-range Canaries 7-day couple budget €1,800–2,800 excl flights.
quote: Lanzarote and Fuerteventura occupy middle ground with abundant mid-range options at €60-100 nightly... mid-range travelers €1,500-2,400

claim: Lanzarote 7-day couple trip total typically lands €1,000–2,500 depending on style.
quote: A 7-day trip to Lanzarote costs €1,000–2,500 for couples

claim: Heraklion city bus single ride ~€2; modest local transport budget for Crete with car rental optional.
quote: city zone bus tickets in Heraklion cost from approximately €2 per ride

claim: Belgian (Vlaanderen) autumn school holiday in 2026 runs Monday 2 Nov to Sunday 8 Nov, meaning the last week of October (26-30 Oct) is NOT a Belgian school break.
quote: Herfstvakantie 2026 ... van maandag 2 november 2026 tot en met zondag 8 november 2026 ... week 45

claim: European DST ends Sunday 25 October 2026, clocks go back one hour, making evenings noticeably shorter for the rest of the trip window.
quote: All EU countries and most European non-EU countries will set clocks back one hour on Sunday, October 25, 2026

claim: Calima (Saharan dust) events in the Canary Islands trigger official AEMET alerts, can dramatically reduce visibility and air quality, and disrupt flights.
quote: Calima Alerts and Warnings in Canary Islands - Levels and Protocols

claim: Calima episodes regularly disrupt air travel into the Canary Islands; an April 2026 event caused widespread flight delays.
quote: Calima dust storm causes air travel delays in Canary Islands

claim: Madeira and the Azores have been hit by hurricanes between June and November, with the highest risk in September and October.
quote: Madeira has gotten hurricanes in the past between the months of June and November, with the highest risk typically occurring in September and October

claim: Hurricane Leslie (October 2018) prompted a tropical storm warning for Madeira and passed 325 km north-northwest of the island before hitting Portugal — illustrative of the late-October ex-tropical risk.
quote: A tropical storm watch was issued for Madeira on 11 October ... upgraded to a warning at 03:00 UTC on 12 October ... passed 325 km (202 mi) north-northwest of Madeira Island

claim: On 29-30 October 2024 a DANA / gota fría dropped >300 mm of rain in 4 hours over Valencia, killing hundreds — late October is peak gota fría season for eastern Spain.
quote: On the night of 29-30 October 2024, a DANA event caused considerable loss of life and extensive damage, especially in the Valencian Community ... more than 300 millimetres of rainfall during a 4-hour period

claim: Crete's autumn officially starts in October with the first rains; rain becomes more likely especially in the second half of the month.
quote: October is the start of the Cretan autumn and will almost always have some rain, sometimes heavy

claim: Many Greek-island resort hotels and beach clubs (e.g. parts of Faliraki on Rhodes, Kos resorts like Lambi/Kardamena) wind down mid-October and have shut by 1 November, while main towns (Rhodes Town, Heraklion, Chania) stay open year-round.
quote: Places catering to tourists will typically close down by November 1st ... Clubs in Faliraki, Rhodes shut down around the 15th October

claim: October is one of Sicily's wettest months with intense showers and sirocco-driven storms; sirocco gusts can reach 100 km/h in autumn.
quote: October is one of the wettest months in Sicily with more than three inches of rain

claim: Sirocco winds reach up to 100 km/h, are most common in autumn and spring, and bring storms and 'blood rain' (red Saharan dust mixed with rain) to southern Italy and Malta.
quote: Sirocco winds with speeds of up to 100 km/h (62 mph) are most common during autumn and spring

claim: Malta's October sees the first proper autumn storms with ~90 mm rain over ~9 days; the gregale wind can hit 90 km/h during these storms.
quote: During storms, the Gregale wind has reached speeds up to 90 km/h

claim: Cyprus in October stays the warmest of the shortlist (25-29 °C, only 2-3 rainy days, ~18 mm in Paphos), the most 'set and forget' weather pick.
quote: October showers in Cyprus are a rarity, with around 2 to 3 rainy days throughout the month

claim: The Algarve stays open all season in October — restaurants, beaches, boat tours all running — with ~80 mm rain over ~10 days, increasing toward month-end.
quote: In October, everything remains open. This is a great time to go hiking, relax on the beach, take a boat or kayaking tour

claim: Madeira in October stays at 20-25 °C with stable trail conditions early in the month, increasing rain risk toward November.
quote: temperatures hover between 20°C and 25°C ... mild and stable weather early in the season and increased chances of rain toward November

claim: Tenerife in October draws growing crowds of northern-European wintering visitors, so prices and bookings rise even though weather stays warm.
quote: October can be a mixed month to visit Tenerife mainly because of the crowds and prices, as it attracts too many people from Europe who come to the island exactly during this month to spend the winter

claim: DANA / gota fría cut-off lows form when warm Mediterranean moisture meets cold upper-air pools — a recurring autumn pattern affecting Spanish-Mediterranean coasts including the Balearics and Costa Blanca.
quote: Cut-off lows, cold drops and DANA
